The '''''Nonnah''''' was a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Transport/Legends|transport]] with a central portion and two spars on the side.<ref name="RS I" /> As part of Alliance operations on marginal worlds, it was carrying [[Soldier/Legends|troops]] and stolen [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] supplies, but was shot down by the Empire around [[0 ABY/Legends|6]] [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the pivotal [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].<ref name="RS I guide" /> Both Rebel and Imperial forces were [[Search for the Nonnah|sent immediately]] to the crash site on [[Chorax]] in an attempt to retrieve the downed craft. The Empire arrived first, but the Rebellion's [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] proved more than a match for the small force. The Rogues' [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|A-wings]] competed with [[Viper probe droid/Legends|viper probe droids]] to find the downed [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. Once the ''Nonnah'' was located in a [[river]] valley, Imperial reinforcements, in the form of a [[Sentinel-class landing craft/Legends|''Sentinel''-class landing craft]] carrying an [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]], [[All Terrain Personal Transport/Legends|AT-PTs]], and [[XR-85 tank droid]]s, along with [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE Bombers]] were dispatched to destroy the ''Nonnah'', but the Rogues were able to fend off all attacks until the arrival of a Rebel rescue shuttle. In a last ditch effort to prevent the Alliance securing the cargo, a squad of [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE Interceptors]] were dispatched to destroy the shuttle escaping the planet's atmosphere, but the timely intervention of [[Rogue Leader/Legends|Rogue Leader]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and his [[Wingmate/Legends|wingmen]] stopped the shuttle from sustaining any serious hull damage.<ref name="RS I" />

{{Ship-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Nonnah'' appeared in the [[1998]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']] and was named after the game's texture artist, Buddy Hannon ("Nonnah" being "Hannon" spelled backwards).<ref name="Kaufman Blog">{{Blog|RyanKaufman|8|What's In a Name?}}</ref>
